accepted.text:
Why use the Claude Agent SDK?
- We realized people were using Claude Code for non-coding tasks.
- We build our agents on top of our Agent SDK.
- It is built on top of lessons we’ve learned deploying Claude Code to millions of users.
- We have strong opinions on the best way to build agents.
- One of our biggest learnings: the Bash tool is the most powerful agent tool.

blocks.text: Why use the Claude Agent SDK?
We realized people were using Claude Code for non-coding tasks.
We build our agents on top of our Agent SDK.
It is built on top of lessons we’ve learned deploying Claude Code to millions of users.
We have strong opinions on the best way to build agents.
One of our biggest learnings: the Bash tool is the most powerful agent tool.

accepted.vision_text:
Why use the Claude Agent SDK?
- We realized people were using Claude Code for non-coding tasks.
- We build our agents on top of our Agent SDK.
- It is built on top of lessons we’ve learned deploying Claude Code to millions of users.
- We have strong opinions on the best way to build agents.
- One of our biggest learnings: the Bash tool is the most powerful agent tool.

accepted.text:
The Agent SDK is the best way to build agents in the “Anthropic Way”
- Unix Primitives: Every agent should use bash [1] & a file system [2] (e.g. skills & memory)
- Agents > Workflows: Agents build their own context + decide what to do with tools.
- Code Generation for non-coding: We use code gen to generate docs, query the web, etc.
- Every agent has a container.
More on this here.

blocks.text: The Agent SDK is the best way to build agents in the “Anthropic Way”
Unix Primitives: Every agent should use bash [1] & a file system [2] (e.g. skills & memory)
Agents > Workflows: Agents build their own context + decide what to do with tools.
Code Generation for non-coding: We use code gen to generate docs, query the web, etc.

accepted.vision_text:
The Agent SDK is the best way to build agents in the “Anthropic Way”
- Unix Primitives: Every agent should use bash [1] & a file system [2] (e.g. skills & memory)
- Agents > Workflows: Agents build their own context + decide what to do with tools.
- Code Generation for non-coding: We use code gen to generate docs, query the web, etc.
- Every agent has a container.
More on this here.

accepted.text:
Bash is all you Need
Bash is what makes Claude Code so good.
Bash is a generic version for “code mode” or programmatic tool calling.
The bash tool allows you to:
- Store the results of tool calls to files, so you can search them.
- Store memory in files.
- Dynamically generate scripts and call them.
- Compose functionality, use unix primitives like tail, grep, cat, etc.
- Use existing powerful software like ffmpeg, libreoffice, etc.

blocks.text: Bash is what makes Claude Code so good.
Bash is a generic version for “code mode” or programmatic tool calling.
The bash tool allows you to:
Store the results of tool calls to files, so you can search them.
Dynamically generate scripts and call them.
Compose functionality, use unix primitives like tail, grep, cat, etc.
Use existing powerful software like ffmpeg, libreoffice, etc.

accepted.vision_text:
Bash is all you Need
Bash is what makes Claude Code so good.
Bash is a generic version for “code mode” or programmatic tool calling.
The bash tool allows you to:
- Store the results of tool calls to files, so you can search them.
- Store memory in files.
- Dynamically generate scripts and call them.
- Compose functionality, use unix primitives like tail, grep, cat, etc.
- Use existing powerful software like ffmpeg, libreoffice, etc.

accepted.text: Workflows & Agents

blocks.text: We build both agents and workflows on the Claude agent SDK.
Agents are like Claude Code - You talk to them in natural language and they take action.
Workflows are like our Github action - You define inputs and outputs, e.g. take in a PR and give a code review.

accepted.text: Tools vs Bash vs Code Generation

blocks.text: Tools vs Bash vs Code Generation
Cons: High context usage, not composable
Pros: Composable, static scripts, low context usage
Cons: Longer discovery time, slightly lower call rate
Cons: Needs linting & possibly compilation, careful API design

accepted.vision_text: Tools vs Bash vs Code Generation

accepted.text: Why Skills?

blocks.text: Skills let one agent accomplish longer, more complex tasks without needing Subagents. The agent can use many Skills and read them only when needed.

accepted.text: What are Skills?

blocks.text: An organized collection of files (instructions, executable code, assets) containing everything Claude needs for a specific task.
1. General capabilities Claude isn't good at out of the box (yet)
2. Knowledge of an organization's workflows and best practices
